witnesses.description: A three-point discrete topology with finite open-cover witnesses.

checks.claim: The listed finite open cover has a listed finite subcover.
checks.notes: The validator checks that the cover and subcover use open sets and both cover the finite universe.

checks.claim: The listed finite cover has no one-set subcover, and the listed two-set subcover covers the universe.
checks.notes: The validator enumerates all smaller subfamilies and confirms none cover the universe.

checks.claim: The listed closed family has the finite intersection property and total intersection {b}.
checks.notes: The validator checks closedness and enumerates non-empty finite subfamily intersections.

checks.claim: The listed family {{a}, {b}} is not an open cover of {a,b,c}.

data.certificate: solve_with_drat_proof emits a DRAT refutation for c_covered = false and c_covered = true, elaborate_drat_to_lrat emits LRAT, and check_drat/check_lrat independently recheck both proof objects

checks.notes: The validator recomputes the cover union and rejects the missing point c. The DIMACS artifact isolates the false c-covered claim, and Axeyum emits checked DRAT/LRAT evidence.

checks.claim: General compactness and finite-intersection-property theorems require future Lean resources.

data.target_theorem_shape: every open cover has a finite subcover, or equivalently every closed family with the finite intersection property has nonempty total intersection
data.future_checker: Lean module with no sorryAx dependencies

checks.notes: The finite rows are checked; arbitrary topological compactness remains a proof-assistant horizon.
